2606.29808.title: Making Multimodal LLMs Reliable Chart Data Extractors: A Benchmark and Training Framework
2606.29808.paper_summary: Chart data extraction, which reverse-engineers data tables from chart images, is essential for reproducibility, analysis, retrieval, and redesign. Existing interactive tools are reliable but tedious, and mixed-initiative systems, while more efficient, lack generalizability. Recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) offer a unified interface for chart interpretation, yet their ability to extract accurate data tables, especially without visible labels, remains unclear. We build a benchmark featuring diverse real-world charts without data labels to evaluate this capability. Results show that, while current MLLMs reliably reconstruct table structures, they struggle with precise value recovery. To address this, we revisit chart data extraction from a human-centered perspective and argue that extraction should follow a progressive learning process similar to how people read charts. Our training framework substantially improves numerical accuracy, achieving state-of-the-art performance with a 7B-parameter model. A user study further shows that our model effectively supports mixed-initiative workflows for reliable chart data extraction.
2606.29808.paper_summary_zh: 圖表數據提取，即從圖表圖像中反向工程數據表，是重現性、分析、檢索和重新設計的關鍵。現有的互動工具雖然可靠，但過於繁瑣，而混合主動系統雖然更高效，但缺乏通用性。最近的多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）提供了統一的圖表解釋介面，但它們提取準確數據表的能力，尤其是在沒有可見標籤的情況下，仍然不明確。我們建立了一個基準，包含多樣的現實世界圖表，且不帶數據標籤，以評估這一能力。結果顯示，雖然當前的MLLMs能可靠地重建表格結構，但在精確值恢復方面存在困難。為了解決這個問題，我們從以人為中心的角度重新審視圖表數據提取，並主張提取應遵循類似於人們閱讀圖表的漸進學習過程。我們的訓練框架顯著提高了數值準確性，使用7B參數模型達到了最先進的性能。一項用戶研究進一步顯示，我們的模型有效支持混合主動工作流程以可靠地提取圖表數據。
2606.29808.author: Yuchen He et.al.
2606.29808.authors: Yuchen He, Peizhi Ying, Liqi Cheng, Kuilin Peng, Yuan Tian, Dazhen Deng, Yingcai Wu
